data.title: Indian fintech firm Pine Labs lists at 9.5% premium in early trading
data.description:
Shares of Indian fintech firm Pine Labs (PINL.NS) listed at a premium of nearly 10% on the National Stock Exchange of India in pre-open trade on Friday, after a $440 million initial public offering.

The stock listed at 242 rupees, above the issue price of 221 rupees.

data.title: India's Muthoot Finance hits record high on Q2 profit surge
data.description:
** Shares of Muthoot Finance rise 9.4% to a record high of 3,711 rupees

** Gold loan financier's Q2 profit jumps 87.5% on strong loan demand amid soaring prices for the precious metal

** MUTT also raises fiscal 2026 gold loan growth guidance to 30%-35% from 15% driven by favorable regulatory changes, higher gold prices

** At least four analysts raise their price target on the "buy"-rated stock, while maintaing up their rating - data compiled by LSEG

** Median PT now at xx rupees vs xx rupees last month

** Motilal Oswal ("Neutral", TP: 3,800 rupees) expects gold loan growth of ~38% in FY26, raises FY26/FY27 EPS estimates by ~10% each to factor in higher loan growth and sustenance of current NIMs in next fiscal

** Bernstein ("Outperform", TP: 3,400 rupees) says MUTT's consol AUM growth remained strong

** MUTT up ~74% YTD

data.title: TENGKU PERMAISURI NORASHIKIN URGES PR PRACTITIONERS TO FOSTER UNITY, TRUST IN DIGITAL ERA
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 14 (Bernama) — Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin has called on public relations practitioners to play a pivotal role in fostering unity and trust in an era of rapid change and digital transformation.

Her Royal Highness said that in today’s world, shaped by uncertainty and rapid change, communication is more critical than ever as it enables societies to find common ground, leaders to inspire hope and communities to face shared challenges.

“In this era of constant information flow, the stories we tell and the values we uphold will determine how the world sees our region — not as a collection of countries, but as a community built on mutual respect, shared responsibility and enduring hope,” she said at the Global Public Relations Conference and Festival Malaysia 2025 (GPRCFMY 2025) here today.

Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin highlighted that, as artificial intelligence, data and digital tools reshape the landscape of communication, technology is only as effective as the intent behind it.

Her Royal Highness added that true progress lies not in algorithms but in authenticity, not in speed but in sincerity and not in visibility but in values.

-- MORE

TENGKU PERMAISURI-PUBLIC RELATIONS 2 (LAST) KUALA LUMPUR

“Let every message we craft be anchored in honesty and humanity. Let every campaign we build promote not division, but understanding and let every platform we create serve not vanity but value.

“For communication, when guided by conscience, can heal wounds, rebuild trust and renew hope,” said Her Royal Highness.

At the event, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin also launched the PRactitioners Website and the Code of Ethics and Integrity in Public Relations.

Organised by the Public Relations Practitioners Society of Malaysia (PRactitioners), the inaugural three-day event, which began yesterday, brings together more than 500 communication leaders, media professionals, policymakers and academics from Malaysia and abroad.

With the theme ‘Humanity Amplified: Redefining Public Relations in the Age of Intelligence’, the conference explores how trust, ethics and technology can coexist in modern communications through dialogues, masterclasses and plenary sessions designed to advance global communication excellence.

-- BERNAMA

TAGS: Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, public relations, GPRCFMY 2025, digital

data.title: KYIV TO AUDIT STATE-OWNED COMPANIES AMID CORRUPTION SCANDAL
data.description:
KYIV, Nov 14 (Bernama-dpa) — Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko on Thursday announced a "comprehensive audit" of all major state-owned companies amid a widening corruption scandal in the energy and defence sectors, reported German Press Agency (dpa).

Companies in these two sectors will be subject to strict checks, Svyrydenko said on Telegram. The focus will be on transparency in government procurement and financial control. She specifically mentioned the oil and gas company Naftogaz and Ukrainian railways.

In a second post, the prime minister said that the tender for the top job at Ukraine's Gas Transmission System Operator would also be suspended since one of the finalists was mentioned in the investigations by corruption authorities. 

"Under these circumstances, continuing the tender process is not compatible with the principles of transparency, integrity and trust in the process," she wrote.

Oksana Kryvenko, who worked as an adviser to former energy and justice minister German Galushchenko, had previously made it through the pre-selection process. 

Galushchenko is one of two ministers who had to resign amid what Ukrainian officials describe as a far-reaching investigation into alleged bribery linked to energy infrastructure projects.

The National Anti-Corruption Bureau (NABU) and the Specialised Anti-Corruption Prosecutor's Office (SAP) said they are probing alleged kickbacks involving state nuclear company Energoatom.

The case centres on millions in bribes allegedly paid during the construction of protective structures meant to shield energy facilities from Russian airstrikes.

-- BERNAMA-dpa

TAGS: Kyiv, audit, state-owned companies, corruption

data.title: AEROTRAIN ACTION PLAN TO BE ACTIVATED TOMORROW - MAHB
data.description:
SEPANG, Nov 14 (Bernama) — Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) will activate a month-long comprehensive action plan (CAP) starting tomorrow to enhance the reliability and resilience of the KL International Airport Aerotrain system, leveraging the remaining period of the defects liability period (DLP) to address system vulnerabilities through a structured, multi-stage approach.

MAHB managing director Datuk Mohd Izani Ghani said with the concurrence of the Transport Ministry and the acknowledgement of the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD), the plan will commence with daily inspections and rectification works scheduled from 9pm to 7am to minimise passenger disruption.

The plan represents a decisive step forward in restoring public confidence through a structured approach, regulatory collaboration and open communication, he said.

"Our focus remains on ensuring that the Aerotrain delivers the reliability and safety expected at an international gateway such as KLIA.

"This plan allows us to systematically address the root causes of past disruptions while reaffirming our commitment to passenger safety and service excellence," he told a media briefing here today.

Mohd Izani said during this period, full bus services will be deployed to ensure uninterrupted connectivity between the main terminal and the satellite buildings.

"For greater passenger comfort, more flight departures and arrivals will take place at the main terminal building to reduce the need for transfers," he added.

Developed in collaboration with appointed contractors and consultants, the CAP comprises three structured stages to address system vulnerabilities in a systematic and verifiable manner, he said.

The focus in stage one will be to identify system imperfections, resolve root-cause issues, execute planned technical and software upgrades and conduct detailed inspections of the complete train system, Mohd Izani said.

Stage two centres on validation involving simulated operational testing and targeted enhancements such as software updates, component recalibration and real-time stress testing to reinforce system robustness, he said.

"The final stage will involve monitored trial operations to confirm system readiness and operational stability before full service resumption," he said.

An independent assessment will be undertaken to conduct comprehensive technical and operational audits for further assurance, he said.

APAD will perform a formal safety inspection upon the plan’s completion.

On the possibility of extending the CAP after Dec 15, Mohd Izani said this will be decided after stages one and two are completed.

"We have yet to know how many days will be required for trial operations. We will periodically update the media if we need to extend the duration," he added.

Since operations resumed on July 1, the KLIA Aerotrain system has served about seven million passengers and completed over 53,000 return trips, recording an operational service availability of 98.41 per cent.

-- BERNAMA 

TAGS: MAHB, CAP, Aerotrain, system, inspection

data.title: IHERB LAUNCHES MONTH-LONG BLACK FRIDAY EVENT WITH GLOBAL DISCOUNTS UP TO 65 PCT
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 14 (Bernama) — iHerb, an online retailer for health and wellness products, has kicked off its annual Black Friday sales campaign, launching a month-long promotion that will offer escalating discounts to customers worldwide on vitamins, supplements, beauty products, sports nutrition and other wellness essentials.

The promotion begins immediately with pre-Black Friday discounts starting at 20 per cent off across top categories, with savings increasing significantly during the week of Black Friday and Cyber Monday, when deals start at 25 per cent off sitewide.

iHerb in a statement said the month-long event is designed to meet growing international demand, as cross-border e-commerce for Black Friday and Cyber Monday in 2024 increased by more than 40 per cent compared to the previous year, reflecting the event’s global reach for health and wellness essentials.

iHerb Chief Revenue Officer, Hyeyoung Moon said the promotion offers customers a season of discovery and convenience, allowing shoppers worldwide to stock up on wellness essentials and select high-quality holiday gifts at their own pace.

This year’s Black Friday event features weekly deal drops with new categories and brands, a curated Holiday Gift Guide, and an exclusive “12 Days of Favorites” gift set at 50 per cent off standard retail prices. iHerb’s Specials page will also offer discounts of up to 65 per cent on selected products while supplies last.

In addition, iHerb eGift Cards are available, providing recipients with access to a wide range of products on the platform. The digital cards never expire and allow personalised messages and scheduled delivery for convenience.

The month-long Black Friday celebration aims to provide international shoppers with ample time to secure wellness essentials and meaningful gifts, highlighting the company’s commitment to global customer engagement.

-- BERNAMA

data.title: CHINA BECOMES GULF COUNTRIES' LARGEST TRADING PARTNER: UK THINK TANK
data.description:
LONDON, Nov 14 (Bernama-Xinhua) — China has overtaken the United States (US), Britain, and the Eurozone combined to become the Gulf region's largest trading partner, according to a report released Thursday by the London-based think tank Asia House, reported Xinhua.

The report, Middle East Pivot to Asia: The Gulf's Rise as a Global Middle Power, shows that China's trade with the six Gulf countries reached US$257 billion in 2024, narrowly surpassing the US$256 billion in combined Gulf trade with major Western economies.

Asia House projects that Gulf-China trade will climb to US$375 billion by 2028, widening the trade gap with Western economies to US$75 billion.

According to Asia House, China now accounts for 47 per cent of all Gulf trade with emerging Asian economies, with expanding synergies boosting cooperation across non-oil sectors, including renewables, construction, and technology.

The report highlights the United Arab Emirates' (UAE) growing non-oil trade with China, which rose 15 per cent year-on-year in the first half of 2025.

The UAE and Saudi Arabia remain China's largest trading partners in the Gulf, accounting for 46 per cent and 38 per cent, respectively, of China's trade with the bloc.

--BERNAMA-XINHUA 

TAGS: Think tank, report, China, Gulf region’s, largest, trading partner

data.title: Trade agreements between the US and its trading partners, including Malaysia, helped ease tariff-related uncertainties and improve sentiment, further supporting the ringgit’s performance - BNM Governor
Malaysia Q3 FDI Rebounds from 5-Year Low
data.description:
Malaysia’s foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows jumped to MYR 8.47 billion in Q3 2025, rebounding sharply from MYR 1.61 billion in the previous quarter, which had marked the lowest level in nearly five years.

The bulk of the investments flowed into the services sector, especially information & communication and financial services, followed by mining and quarrying.

Key sources of FDI included Singapore, China, and Japan.

data.title: RAJA OF PERLIS OUTLINES FIVE CORE MANDATES TO GUIDE UNISIRAJ’S FUTURE
data.description:
KANGAR, Nov 14 (Bernama) — Raja of Perlis Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail has outlined five core mandates to guide the entire community of Universiti Islam Antarabangsa Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin (UniSIRAJ) as it advances as an institution rooted in truth, knowledge and sincerity, and remains under the protection and blessings of Allah SWT.

Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said the first mandate is to place the appreciation of sunnah at the heart of all university endeavours; the second, to nurture knowledgeable, ethical and well-mannered individuals; the third, to elevate UniSIRAJ as a leading Islamic reference centre across multiple disciplines.

The fourth mandate focuses on strengthening research and innovation based on Islamic values, while the fifth calls for enhancing UniSIRAJ’s role in the development of civilisation and humanity.

“These five mandates must serve as the compass for the university’s journey. When the foundations of sunnah, knowledge and trust are united, we will see the rise of a generation that excels not only in scholarship but also in noble character.

“With Allah’s permission and care, UniSIRAJ will continue to flourish as a beacon of knowledge, a centre of wisdom and a light of civilisation for Perlis, ‘Where Lovers of the Sunnah Are Born’,” he said.

-- MORE

PERLIS RAJA-UNISIRAJ 2 (LAST) KANGAR

The Raja of Perlis delivered the mandates when proclaiming UniSIRAJ as the state’s new beacon of knowledge at Dewan 2020 here last night. Also present was the Raja Perempuan Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Almarhum Tengku Abdul Rashid.

The Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail, and Raja Puan Muda Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil, also graced the ceremony.

Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said that amid global ideological conflict and growing spiritual emptiness, UniSIRAJ must embody the true meaning of rahmatan lil-‘alamin by serving as a bridge between the Islamic world and the international communities, spreading messages of compassion, peace and humanitarian responsibility through knowledge and practice.

UniSIRAJ was formerly known as Kolej Universiti Islam Perlis (KUIPs) before being upgraded to university status. On Nov 23 last year, Bernama reported that Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra, then KUIPs Chancellor, announced that the Higher Education Department, via a letter on Nov 15, 2024, has approved KUIPs’ elevation to a university.

At the ceremony, Prof Dr Muhamad Rozaimi Ramle, previously KUIPs rector, was proclaimed the first vice-chancellor of UniSIRAJ.

-- BERNAMA

TAGS: Raja of Perlis, mandates, UniSIRAJ, sunnah, knowledge, civilisation, humanity, Islamic values
